authority_membrane.anti_claim: Human/agent glosses, first commands, family labels, and standalone/wired notes are navigation and comprehension metadata only. They do not encode evidence strength (see core/organ_evidence_classes.json), score-based progress, maturity, readiness, product completeness, release, private-root equivalence, proof correctness, provider-call, secret-export, or whole-system-correctness authority.

local_semantic_excerpt.exports: bounded owned-source excerpts behind explicit local authority
local_semantic_excerpt.requires: source_body_export_authorized is False even when active

presence_only.exports: names, public glosses, counts, refs, ceilings

source_span_escalation.exports: exact path + symbol pointers only; open source to mutate or prove

authority_membrane.boundary: Plain-language comprehension layer over the accepted public runtime organ registry. Each gloss is distilled from the organ's paper module and adversarially verified against its claim ceiling; glosses are projection metadata, not source authority.

deferred_edges.missing: theorem -> lemma -> tactic edges inside a proof organ
a route node fanning one entry point across multiple organs
a first-class claim node distinct from the per-organ ceiling

local_semantic_excerpt.exports: bounded owned-source excerpts behind explicit local authority
local_semantic_excerpt.requires: source_body_export_authorized is False even when active

presence_only.exports: names, public glosses, counts, refs, ceilings

source_span_escalation.exports: exact path + symbol pointers only; open source to mutate or prove

open_source_when: you must mutate the organ's owned code
you must prove or reproduce a claim from source
the read pack marks a needs_spot_review or unresolved ref
read_me_first: Microcosm is a 82-organ self-describing substrate; each organ is a bounded capability with a runner, a validator command, an authority ceiling, and emitted receipts.
Custody truth: 81/82 organ runners are EXACT-COPY macro bodies -- comprehend them via registry metadata + receipts, not runner source.
Every read here is source-body-free (presence_only); nothing in this packet authorizes release, source export, or whole-system correctness.
7 organ families. This packet IS the hub: open --slice cluster --family <f> for one family, --organ <id> for one organ, --slice math/claims/flows for proof/claim/flow.

recommended_drilldowns.question: read a file's atoms without opening source?

summary.what_not_to_trust: Human/agent glosses, first commands, family labels, and standalone/wired notes are navigation and comprehension metadata only. They do not encode evidence strength (see core/organ_evidence_classes.json), score-based progress, maturity, readiness, product completeness, release, private-root equivalence, proof correctness, provider-call, secret-export, or whole-system-correctness authority.
summary.what_this_is: Plain-language comprehension layer over the accepted public runtime organ registry. Each gloss is distilled from the organ's paper module and adversarially verified against its claim ceiling; glosses are projection metadata, not source authority.

tail_recap.core_frame: 82 organs, 7 families; 81/82 runners are exact-copy macro bodies; presence_only; authorizes nothing.

target_reader: cold Type A / cold codebase reader

thin_or_projection_surfaces.how_to_probe: For any organ run --slice claims --organ <id> to see its validator_command + receipts; an algorithmic_projection organ asserts projection mechanics only, not domain truth.
thin_or_projection_surfaces.note: Where to be skeptical: these are projection / import surfaces, not deep domain capability.
